description: Letter from Yosef b. Yehuda b. Simḥa (Alexandria) to Abū Zikrī Yaḥyā b. Nissim (Fustat), ca. 1050. The writer mentions a number of deals in beads and pearls. He is worried about owing money to a number of people including Yaḥyā b. Nissim and, although they do not demand payment, is anxious to settle the issue. He also mentions having heard about diseases (amrāḍ) that have spread in Fustat. The address is written in both Judaeo-Arabic and Arabic script. (Information from Gil.)
tags: beads, debt, epidemic: amrad, epidemic, Illness, Arabic address, pearls, illness letter 969-1517, medicine
description_he: מכתב מיוסף בן יהודה בן שמחה (אלכסנדריה) לאבו זיכרי יחיא בן נסים (פוסטאט); כ-1050 לספירה; מזכיר עסקאות בחרוזים ופנינים; חושש לחובות לאנשים שונים כולל יחיא בן נסים; שמע על מחלות שפשטו בפוסטאט
